title: p-adic Values of G-functions and Unlikely Intersections
speaker: Georgios Papas
abstract: The Zilber-Pink conjecture is a far reaching and widely open conjecture in the area of "unlikely intersections" generalizing many previous results in the area, such as the recently established André-Oort conjecture. Recently the ``G-functions method'' of Y. André has been able to consistently establish the missing arithmetic result needed to establish cases of this conjecture for Shimura varieties. I will discuss how, using properties of the p-adic values of G-functions, we can get new insights on this conjecture in 2
tags: Workshop on Special Cycles and Related Topics

proof_stages.label: Zilber–Pink motivation and height-reduction strategy
proof_stages.summary: The talk starts from the Zilber–Pink expectation for curves in Shimura varieties and explains that known finiteness results reduce arithmetically to bounding heights of points whose fibers have unexpectedly large endomorphism algebra.

proof_stages.label: Generic abelian-surface families and star points
proof_stages.summary: After imposing genericity of the moduli map, the problem is specialized to principally polarized abelian surfaces, where the relevant unlikely special curves split into QM, square, and elliptic-product types, leading to the same-type “star point” height question relative to a fixed star point.

proof_stages.label: André G-functions and the zero/height estimate
proof_stages.summary: A local parameter at the fixed point and the Gauss–Manin connection produce a matrix of G-functions, and André's notion of global nontrivial value relations gives an effective height bound for algebraic points where such bounded-degree relations occur.

proof_stages.label: Local comparison identifications around the base point
proof_stages.summary: The analytic, flat, de Rham, and Betti or crystalline comparison identifications express the locally defined G-function matrix as the change-of-basis or period matrix for the family near the chosen fiber.

proof_stages.label: Extra endomorphisms produce global relations
proof_stages.summary: For a same-type star point sufficiently close to the base point at a place W, the extra endomorphism yields commutative cohomological diagrams whose period equations translate into a nontrivial global polynomial relation R_{s,W} among the G-function values; with suitable bases this relation is independent of W away from supersingular reduction.

proof_stages.label: Height and finiteness bound modulo supersingular proximity
proof_stages.summary: Combining the placewise relations with André's height estimate controls star points of the fixed type, leaving only a finite or explicitly bounded set of supersingular proximity primes as the obstruction under the stated genericity and good-reduction hypotheses.
